steps.title: Set the equations equal
steps.content: At an intersection, the two expressions for $y$ are equal.$$2x^2-23x+60=x+a$$

steps.title: Use the one-intersection condition
steps.content: Move all terms to one side.$$2x^2-24x+(60-a)=0$$For the graphs to intersect at exactly one point, this quadratic has one solution. That solution occurs at the vertex, whose $x$-coordinate is $$\frac{-(-24)}{2(2)}=6$$The answer is C.
